human_verification.notes: Reviewed broadcast scheduling outputs for boundary discipline, rundown and call-sheet specificity, rights and caption gating, capacity-tradeoff handling, and cross-platform schedule readiness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to recommendation-only broadcast scheduling, prerequisite validation, capacity and sequence analysis, booking-readiness review, and escalation packaging.

Authoritative sources cover broadcast operational obligations, accessibility, production labor and scheduling practice, shoot-day context, traffic exchange, and delivery-package standards with refresh discipline.
Research scenario families confirm industry-specific evidence gaps, anti-generic boundary handling, adjacent-lane escalation, capacity shortfall, tie-break conflict, and final-approval refusal.
Evaluation scenario set covers missing artifacts, capacity overload, conflicting priorities, rights and labor boundaries, late delivery, policy conflicts, and tenant adaptation.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.
Boundary handling remained strong across rights, labor, accessibility, broadcast operations, capacity, and approval-refusal cases.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
